title: Sree Andalurkavu
tagline: Sree Andalurkavu — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Andalurkavu is a very ancient, famous and prominent Thiyyar Community Urayima temple in Andalur in Dharmadam village of Kannur district, Kerala, India.
overview.description: Andalurkavu is a very ancient, famous and prominent Thiyyar Community Urayima temple in Andalur in Dharmadam village of Kannur district, Kerala, India. This ancient temple is in the name of Lord Rama and the main festival is celebrated in mid-February: the first week of the month "Kumbam" of the Malayalam calendar. The administrative authority of this temple (known as the Kottil) belonged to six Thiyyar ancestral houses (Tharavadu) held by the "Four Thara Achans." This center of power functioned as an autonomous region. A unique tradition exists here, when a Thiyyar boy is born in these four r

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Dharmadom
topPlaces.description: Dharmadom or Dharmadam is a Census Town in Kannur district in the state of Kerala, India. This town is located in between Anjarakandi River and Ummanchira River, and Palayad town and Arabian Sea.

topPlaces.name: Palayad
topPlaces.description: Palayad is a census town in Thalassery Taluk in the Indian state of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Sri Chirakkakavu Bhagavthi Temple
topPlaces.description: Sree Chirakkakavu Bhagavathi Temple is a Hindu temple At Thalassery, North Malabar in the state of Kerala, India. It is one of the oldest Devi Temples in North Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Nettur
topPlaces.description: Nettur is a village segment which comes under the jurisdiction of Thalassery municipality, in the state of Kerala, India. It is situated in North Thalassery en route to Kannur via NH 17.

topPlaces.name: Gundert Museum
topPlaces.description: Gundert Museum is a heritage museum located at Thalassery, Kerala. It was originally the residence of Hermann Gundert, a German missionary who is remembered for his contributions to the Malayalam language.

topPlaces.name: Muzhappilangad
topPlaces.description: Muzhappilangad (മുഴപ്പിലങ്ങാട്) is a coastal census town in Kannur Municipal Corporation in Kannur district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is a suburb of Kannur city.

topPlaces.name: Anjarakandi River
topPlaces.description: The Anjarakandi River is one of two major rivers that flows through the Kannur District of Kerala in India. The river originates from the slopes of the Western Ghats bordering Wayanad and Kannur districts.

topPlaces.name: Pinarayi
topPlaces.description: Pinarayi is a census town in Kannur district in the Indian state of Kerala.

The Anjarakandi River is one of two major rivers that flows through the Kannur District of Kerala in India. The river originates from the slopes of the Western Ghats bordering Wayanad and Kannur districts.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sree Andalurkavu?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sree Andalurkavu is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sree Andalurkavu?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Calicut International Airport, Kozhikode (90 km) and the nearest railway station is Mangaluru Central (138 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sree Andalurkavu?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sree Andalurkavu at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sree Andalurkavu?
faq.a: Stays in Sree Andalurkavu start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sree Andalurkavu?
faq.a: The top attractions are Dharmadom, Palayad, Sri Chirakkakavu Bhagavthi Temple, Nettur, Gundert Museum.
